AnalysisG
  • API Reference
  • Plotting Module
  • View page source

Plotting Module

The plotting module provides a high-level interface for creating physics plots using boost-histogram and mplhep.

Overview

The plotting module (src/AnalysisG/core/plotting.pyx) offers:

  • Object-oriented plotting interface

  • Integration with boost-histogram

  • ATLAS/CMS style presets via mplhep

  • Automatic histogram binning

  • Support for 1D and 2D histograms

  • Statistical error handling

  • Easy figure export

Basic Usage

Creating Histograms

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram

# Create 1D histogram
hist = Histogram()
hist.Title = "Jet $p_T$"
hist.xTitle = "$p_T$ [GeV]"
hist.yTitle = "Events"
hist.xBins = 50
hist.xMin = 0
hist.xMax = 500

# Fill histogram
for event in events:
    for jet in event.jets:
        hist.Fill(jet.pt)

# Draw histogram
hist.Draw()
hist.SaveFigure("jet_pt.png")

2D Histograms

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram2D

# Create 2D histogram
hist2d = Histogram2D()
hist2d.Title = "Jet $\eta$ vs $\phi$"
hist2d.xTitle = "$\eta$"
hist2d.yTitle = "$\phi$"
hist2d.xBins = 50
hist2d.yBins = 50

# Fill histogram
for event in events:
    for jet in event.jets:
        hist2d.Fill(jet.eta, jet.phi)

# Draw as heatmap
hist2d.Draw()
hist2d.SaveFigure("jet_eta_phi.png")

API Reference

Histogram Class

class Histogram

1D histogram class.

Properties

Title: str

Histogram title.

xTitle: str

X-axis title with LaTeX support.

yTitle: str

Y-axis title with LaTeX support.

xBins: int

Number of bins on x-axis.

xMin: float

Minimum value on x-axis.

xMax: float

Maximum value on x-axis.

Color: str

Histogram color (matplotlib color string).

LineWidth: float

Line width for histogram outline.

Alpha: float

Transparency (0-1).

Style: str

Histogram style: “step”, “fill”, “errorbar”.

Methods

Fill(x: float, weight: float = 1.0)

Fill histogram with a value.

Parameters:

  • x (float) – Value to fill

  • weight (float) – Event weight

Draw()

Draw the histogram.

Creates or updates the current figure with the histogram.

SaveFigure(filename: str, dpi: int = 300)

Save figure to file.

Parameters:

  • filename (str) – Output filename

  • dpi (int) – Resolution in dots per inch

Supported formats: PNG, PDF, SVG, EPS

Clear()

Clear histogram contents.

Normalize(norm: float = 1.0)

Normalize histogram to given value.

Parameters:

norm (float) – Normalization value

GetIntegral() → float

Get integral of histogram.

Returns:

Sum of all bin contents

Return type:

float

Histogram2D Class

class Histogram2D

2D histogram class.

Properties

Title: str

Histogram title.

xTitle: str

X-axis title.

yTitle: str

Y-axis title.

xBins: int

Number of bins on x-axis.

yBins: int

Number of bins on y-axis.

xMin: float

Minimum value on x-axis.

xMax: float

Maximum value on x-axis.

yMin: float

Minimum value on y-axis.

yMax: float

Maximum value on y-axis.

ColorMap: str

Colormap name (e.g., “viridis”, “plasma”, “hot”).

Methods

Fill(x: float, y: float, weight: float = 1.0)

Fill histogram with values.

Parameters:

  • x (float) – X value

  • y (float) – Y value

  • weight (float) – Event weight

Draw(style: str = 'colormesh')

Draw the 2D histogram.

Parameters:

style (str) – Drawing style: “colormesh”, “contour”, “contourf”

SaveFigure(filename: str, dpi: int = 300)

Save figure to file.

Clear()

Clear histogram contents.

Advanced Features

ATLAS Style Plots

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram
import mplhep

# Use ATLAS style
mplhep.style.use(mplhep.style.ATLAS)

hist = Histogram()
hist.Title = "ATLAS Simulation"
# ... configure and fill histogram ...
hist.Draw()

Stacked Histograms

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Create multiple histograms
signal = Histogram()
background1 = Histogram()
background2 = Histogram()

# Fill histograms...

# Draw stacked
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
signal.Draw()
background1.Draw()
background2.Draw()

plt.legend(["Signal", "Background 1", "Background 2"])
plt.savefig("stacked.png")

Ratio Plots

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.gridspec import GridSpec

data = Histogram()
mc = Histogram()

# Fill histograms...

# Create figure with ratio plot
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8, 8))
gs = GridSpec(2, 1, height_ratios=[3, 1], hspace=0)

# Main plot
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0])
data.Draw()
mc.Draw()
ax1.legend(["Data", "MC"])

# Ratio plot
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1], sharex=ax1)
ratio = data.GetIntegral() / mc.GetIntegral()
ax2.plot([data.xMin, data.xMax], [ratio, ratio])
ax2.set_ylabel("Data/MC")

plt.savefig("ratio_plot.png")

Error Bars

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram

hist = Histogram()
hist.Style = "errorbar"  # Show error bars

# Fill histogram...
hist.Draw()

Custom Styling

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram

hist = Histogram()
hist.Color = "blue"
hist.LineWidth = 2.0
hist.Alpha = 0.7
hist.Style = "fill"

# Configure bins
hist.xBins = 100
hist.xMin = 0
hist.xMax = 1000

# Labels with LaTeX
hist.xTitle = r"$p_T$ [GeV]"
hist.yTitle = r"$\frac{dN}{dp_T}$ [GeV$^{-1}$]"

Integration with boost-histogram

The plotting module uses boost-histogram for efficient histogram operations:

from AnalysisG.core.plotting import Histogram
import boost_histogram as bh

hist = Histogram()
# Internal boost-histogram object accessible
boost_hist = hist._histogram

Performance

The plotting module is optimized for:

  • Fast filling (boost-histogram backend)

  • Efficient memory usage

  • Large datasets (millions of events)

  • Batch plotting operations

See Also

  • boost-histogram documentation

  • mplhep documentation

  • matplotlib documentation
